title: Phoenix Playbook
description: Schema for a phoenix playbook — the structured reconstruction plan for a failed node. A phoenix playbook captures the failed node's identity (VMIDs, IPs, hostnames, cluster role) and how to reconstitute it on new or repaired hardware. Generated by the Assessment Engine from bootstrap-state.json and cluster state. Executed by phoenix.sh.

identity.description: The node's preserved identity — what must come back exactly as it was

lan_ip.description: LAN IP (must be preserved for cluster membership)

vmids.description: All VMIDs that must be reserved on the rebuilt node

bridge_names.description: Bridge names that must be recreated (VMs reference these by name)

zfs_pool_name.description: ZFS pool name (VM disk paths depend on this)

hardware_profile.description: Hardware profile of the replacement machine (from discover-hardware.py). May differ from original; playbook adapts ZFS topology and bridge layout to actual hardware.

restoration_scope.description: full: restore all services; partial: operator-selected subset; deferred: baseline only

deferred_services.description: Service names deferred to a later restoration pass (restoration_scope=partial)

waves.description: Ordered restoration waves. Each wave must complete before the next begins.

estimated_total_minutes.description: Sum of estimated_minutes across all waves

id.description: Unique step identifier within the wave

action.description: Human-readable description of what this step does

validation.description: Validation commands to run after step completes

method.description: RESTORE=from backup; RECREATE=from IaC; VERIFY=read-only check; CONFIGURE=config change

on_failure.description: What to do if step fails: abort | skip | retry | human

secret_refs.description: KeePass paths needed for this step
